level_signals: 2+ years of experience as a Product Manager
Language suggests contributing to strategy rather than owning it ('contributing to the product strategy')
Role framed as opportunity to 'grow your product management skills'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years of experience as a Product Manager, preferably in the payments or fintech industry.
Experience in launching and managing products or features that have delivered value to users.
A good understanding of the acquiring landscape, payment processing, and industry trends.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working with international teams and diverse cultures.
Familiarity with SQL or other data querying languages.
A passion for payments and a desire to make a significant impact on small businesses.

scope.surface: Acquiring products within SumUp's Acquiring tribe
scope.business_model: B2SMB fintech — card acceptance and payment processing for small merchants
core_responsibilities: Contributing to the product strategy and roadmap for your area, ensuring alignment with overall business goals.
Managing aspects of the end-to-end product lifecycle, from discovery to launch and iteration.
Conducting market research, competitor analysis, and customer feedback analysis to identify opportunities.
Translating user needs and business requirements into detailed product specifications and user stories.
Collaborating closely with engineering, design, and other stakeholders to ensure successful product delivery.
Monitoring and analyzing product performance, identifying areas for improvement, and iterating on existing features.
Communicating product updates, progress, and challenges to relevant stakeholders.

green_flags: Clear product domain (Acquiring) with a well-defined tribe structure
Global fintech company with 3000+ employees across 22 offices — real scale
Explicit encouragement to apply even without meeting 100% of criteria
Individual learning budget and 10 days educational vacation mentioned
Generous vacation policy: 25 days + 1 accumulative day per year of tenure
Sofia office described with specific amenities, suggesting real investment in in-person culture
red_flags: Location (Sofia) is only stated in benefits section, not confirmed explicitly as the role location in main body — mild ambiguity
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization support
Role may be mid-level with limited strategic ownership ('contributing to' rather than 'owning' strategy)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and language around 'contributing to' strategy and 'growing skills')
location.cities_listed (Sofia inferred from benefits section mentioning 'Sofia office'; not stated as role location in job header)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'Sofia office' reference in benefits; no remote option mentioned)
ambiguities: Years requirement bullet says '2+ years of experience as a Product Manager, preferably in the payments or fintech industry' — the 'preferably' qualifier applies to the industry context, not the years requirement; years treated as hard, industry as soft context.
Location: JD body does not explicitly state the role is based in Sofia; it is only mentioned in the benefits section ('innovative Sofia office'). Treated as Sofia/onsite but worth confirming.
